principle: ThinkIt should not hand Ben robot packet sludge. It should say what we are thinking of doing next, who should build it, who should challenge it, and what proof comes back.

workflow.builder_team_note: The Fraggle Rock builders are the Doozers. Here, Doozers means the cowork/build lane: Maker, Dink, and Ender when Ender is cutting, filtering, or shaping work.
workflow.review_team_note: Reviewers do not build first. They sign off, catch drift, name missing proof, kill weak ideas, or ask for a sharper packet.
workflow.thinkit_job: ThinkIt routes the packet, records the decision, shows who answered, and refuses to call SENT success.
perfect_loop: Doozers propose the next concrete build move.
Reviewers sign off, modify, or kill the proposal.
ThinkIt dispatches the chosen work and waits for receiver proof.
Speaker/Brainboot records what changed so the next session starts from shared reality.

lanes.question: Werkles: do we fix the command center, tighten source truth, or clean the merge lane next?
lanes.state: Werkles is the working organism/repo surface. The next move should make ThinkIt more usable or make source truth harder to lose.
lanes.default_mode: Doozers propose a practical build; reviewers decide whether it actually reduces mule work.

moves.title: Make every important ThinkIt button prove what it did.
moves.why: The dashboard has looked alive before while clicks did nothing useful. We need fewer pretty cards and more visible cause/effect.
moves.command: Pick the top five ThinkIt buttons Ben actually uses and make each show: packet created, destination, sent/not sent, received/not received, and next proof gap.
moves.doozer_question: What is the smallest UI/API change that makes these buttons impossible to misunderstand?
moves.review_question: Does this actually prove button action, or does it still hide failure behind local packet creation?
moves.proof_required: Before/after button list, endpoint called by each button, durable path written, and one failed-state example.
moves.human_gate: Ben approves which buttons count as important.

moves.title: Make the shared source-truth map obvious from the dashboard.
moves.why: If the Aeyes cannot find the book, Speaker, relay receipts, and repo truth from one plain place, Ben becomes the map again.
moves.command: Create a human-readable source-truth panel: book folder, Speaker root, Brainboot bootpacks, relay receipts, repo branch, current commit, and what is stale.
moves.doozer_question: What should the dashboard show first so a restarted Aeye knows where truth lives?
moves.review_question: Which listed truth source is still unproven, stale, or too local to be trusted?
moves.proof_required: Panel fields, exact paths/URLs, readback timestamps, and stale/missing flags.
moves.human_gate: Ben gates any claim that a source is canonical.

moves.title: Clean the ThinkIt merge lane without polishing the wrong thing.
moves.why: ThinkIt is now useful enough to keep, but the next merge should not drag dead preview confusion or static cockpit leftovers along with it.
moves.command: Return the smallest merge checklist for ThinkIt: keep, kill, hold, and must-test before the next push.
moves.doozer_question: What files/routes are safe to merge because they are live and useful?
moves.review_question: What looks impressive but should not survive into main?
moves.proof_required: Keep/kill/hold list with exact files or routes and one test command per keep item.

lanes.question: Kind Sir: what should this become in the real world first?
lanes.state: Kind Sir needs one usable offer, one source-backed promise, and one testable next step. No PM-software fog.
lanes.default_mode: Doozers turn the idea into an artifact; reviewers make sure it is not just pretty language.

moves.title: Pull one clean Kind Sir offer out of the book.
moves.why: The book keeps saying something valuable about continuity and human infrastructure. Kind Sir needs one plain offer that a real person can understand.
moves.command: Use the Nerdkle book source as reference and draft one Kind Sir offer: who it helps, what pain it removes, what proof would show it worked.
moves.doozer_question: What artifact can we make today that makes Kind Sir more real?
moves.review_question: Is this offer concrete enough that a human could say yes/no, or is it still philosophy?
moves.proof_required: One offer sentence, one audience, one pain removed, one proof-of-use test.

moves.title: Draw the line between Ben work and machine work.
moves.why: Kind Sir should not sell Ben as the mule. It should define what the machine handles and what remains human judgment.
moves.command: Return a two-column boundary: Ben does / machine does, with one example client/workflow.
moves.doozer_question: What simple template or checklist would remove Ben from repeat labor?
moves.review_question: Where is the boundary dishonest, too automated, or too dependent on Ben?
moves.proof_required: Two-column boundary, one example, one no-go automation line.
moves.human_gate: Ben approves the boundary before it becomes sales language.

moves.title: Pick one tiny Kind Sir test instead of designing the company.
moves.why: A small test beats another sprawling business architecture note.
moves.command: Propose three tiny Kind Sir tests Ben could run this week, then recommend one.
moves.doozer_question: What can we build or package in under one hour?
moves.review_question: Which test gives the clearest signal without creating more work than it removes?
moves.proof_required: Three tests, one recommendation, expected signal, and stop condition.

lanes.question: Nerdkle: what makes the organism more real today?
lanes.state: Nerdkle has relay, book source truth, Speaker pieces, and ThinkIt. The next move should improve shared memory, proof, or routing.
lanes.default_mode: Doozers build the next mechanism; reviewers decide whether it prevents drift or just adds machinery.

moves.title: Make the Doozer proposal / reviewer sign-off loop real.
moves.why: Auto-building without critique becomes drift. Critique without builders becomes theater. Nerdkle needs both in one loop.
moves.command: Wire a first pass where Maker/Dink/Ender propose the next move and Ender/Bean/Thufir/Skybro sign off, modify, or kill it.
moves.doozer_question: What is the smallest route/data shape that lets builders propose work without Ben translating it?
moves.review_question: What sign-off fields prevent bad build momentum: drift, missing proof, wrong owner, wrong source, or no stop condition?
moves.proof_required: One proposal packet, one reviewer packet, one returned decision, and dashboard proof of who answered.
moves.human_gate: Ben gates any automatic execution beyond proposing/routing.

moves.title: Show what every Aeye should know after Brainboot.
moves.why: The root anti-mule move is not Ben remembering the context. Brainboot needs to show current state, source truth, and next work from Speaker.
moves.command: Return the current Brainboot memory map: what is in Speaker, what gets rendered into bootpacks, what each Aeye receives, and what is missing.
moves.doozer_question: What file or endpoint should ThinkIt read to show Brainboot state in plain English?
moves.review_question: What still forces Ben to restate context after a new session starts?
moves.proof_required: Speaker path, bootpack path, current-state source, receiver proof path, and missing/state-stale flags.
moves.human_gate: Ben gates canonical memory edits, not readback.

moves.title: Make chapter writing a round trip, not a pile of files.
moves.why: The book is where the architecture and language meet. It needs chapter text, editor packets, returned reports, and revision decisions in one loop.
moves.command: For the active chapter, return: what to write next, which cousin editors should review it, and what receipt proves the chapter moved forward.
moves.doozer_question: What can ThinkIt package so a writer/editor Aeye can work without Ben pasting source text?
moves.review_question: Does the returned chapter work actually improve the manuscript or just create more commentary?
moves.proof_required: Chapter source path, returned edit/report path, requested cousin packets, and one recommended next chapter action.

lanes.question: Oddly Godly on Spanzee: what can run on AUTO G, and what still needs a leash?
lanes.state: Oddly Godly should become an auto lane only after Spanzee route, command line, proof return, and stop conditions are known.
lanes.default_mode: Doozers map the route; reviewers decide whether Spanzee can safely run without Ben steering every step.

moves.title: Map the Spanzee route before pretending AUTO G exists.
moves.why: If ThinkIt cannot see where packets go and how responses return, Oddly Godly will become another hidden ghost lane.
moves.command: Return the current Spanzee receiver, command surface, repo/folder location if any, and missing access.
moves.doozer_question: What path or command can a builder use without Ben explaining it again?
moves.review_question: What proof is still missing before this lane can run on a timer or AUTO G?
moves.proof_required: Receiver thread, command path, source folder/repo, response path, and blocker list.
moves.human_gate: Ben gates any new remote-control setup.

moves.title: Write the first Oddly Godly work packet like a Bird.
moves.why: The lane needs one mission-carrying packet with context, owner, next action, evidence, destination, and failure condition.
moves.command: Draft the first Oddly Godly Bird packet for Spanzee, but mark any unproven destination as a blocker.
moves.doozer_question: What can be packaged cleanly enough that Spanzee can work without Ben carrying context?
moves.review_question: Does this packet have a real owner, proof requirement, and stop condition?
moves.proof_required: Packet draft plus destination proof or explicit blocker.

moves.title: Define the Oddly Godly AUTO G stop rules.
moves.why: Auto lanes need brakes. AUTO G should mean useful momentum, not uncontrolled task spray.
moves.command: Return GO/PARTIAL/NO-GO conditions for Oddly Godly AUTO G and the exact events that should stop the loop.
moves.doozer_question: What recurring action could safely run once the route is mapped?
moves.review_question: What would prove the lane is drifting, overbuilding, or burning money?
moves.proof_required: GO checklist, stop conditions, proof cadence, and human gate trigger.
moves.human_gate: AUTO G activation and spending/remote changes remain Operator-approved.
